headline: A critical unauthenticated remote-code-execution flaw in Splunk Enterprise drew in-the-wild attacks and a three-day federal patch order, even as a Texas vendor breach spilled three million driver's licenses and an unpatchable silicon exploit cracked older iPhones.

emerging_trends.text: Attackers weaponize freshly disclosed enterprise software within days, driving the unauthenticated Splunk Enterprise bug into active exploitation and a three-day CISA patch deadline almost on arrival.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Splunk Enterprise RCE Under Active Attack

emerging_trends.text: Criminals now launder reputation as readily as they write code, stuffing GitHub stars, YouTube tutorials, and favorable VirusTotal comments to dress crypto-stealing tools as trustworthy software.

Enterprises are confronting AI agents as unmanaged identities that deploy code and reach internal services with little oversight, a blind spot that exploits like AutoJack already turn into host takeover.

topics.title: Splunk Enterprise RCE Under Active Attack
topics.latest_developments: CISA added CVE-2026-20253, a critical unauthenticated remote-code-execution flaw in Splunk Enterprise, to its known exploited vulnerabilities catalog and gave federal civilian agencies until June 21, 2026, to apply mitigations after Splunk and Resecurity confirmed in-the-wild exploitation.
topics.summary: The bug lets a remote attacker run code without authenticating, opening the door to full system compromise of the log-analytics platform many enterprises use as their security backbone. Resecurity urged operators to patch immediately and hunt for indicators of compromise in request logs.

sources.title: Unauthenticated RCE in Splunk Enterprise under active attack (CVE-2026-20253)
CISA: Splunk Enterprise flaw actively exploited, patch by Sunday
Splunk Enterprise Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks Days After Disclosure

topics.title: Klue Supply-Chain Breach Cascades to Salesforce
topics.latest_developments: Huntress published a detailed June 18 post-mortem calling the incident a security domino effect in which one compromised Klue integration credential cascaded into data theft across connected platforms including Salesforce, while Recorded Future joined as a confirmed victim and Salesforce disabled the Klue Battlecards integration.
topics.summary: Klue, a competitive-intelligence platform that wires CRM and sales data across business tools, became the entry point for attackers who exfiltrated data from customers' Salesforce instances. Salesforce customers cannot reconnect the app until further notice.

sources.title: Klue breach lead to Salesforce data theft, Huntress affected
Cybersecurity Firms Impacted by Klue Supply Chain Attack
Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data

topics.title: AI Agents Become Ungoverned Identities
topics.latest_developments: Token Security and BleepingComputer argued that AI agents now access data, trigger workflows, and deploy code with almost no oversight, making them an unmanaged identity class, while The Hacker News reframed shadow AI as an access-control problem rather than a data-leakage one.
topics.summary: Enterprises that bolted AI agents onto production systems gave them broad reach without the identity, credential, and blast-radius controls applied to human accounts. Researchers warn that this gap, the same trust boundary the AutoJack exploit abuses, demands agent governance now.

sources.title: Every AI Agent Is an Identity. Most Organizations Don't Treat Them That Way
Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control

topics.title: Unpatchable usbliter8 Breaks Apple SecureROM
topics.latest_developments: Researchers at Paradigm Shift published usbliter8, a working exploit that achieves arbitrary code execution inside the SecureROM of Apple's A12 and A13 chips, code burned into silicon at manufacture that no software update can reach.
topics.summary: The flaw permanently affects iPhones and iPads built on the A12 and A13 processors for as long as those devices stay in use. It is not a remote attack; it requires physical USB access to the device.

sources.title: Unpatchable 'usbliter8' Exploit Breaks Apple A12 and A13 SecureROM Boot Chain

topics.title: Texas Wildlife Vendor Breach Exposes 3 Million
topics.latest_developments: The Texas Parks and Wildlife Department disclosed a breach at the vendor running its hunting and fishing license system, exposing personal information, including driver's license data, for more than three million individuals.
topics.summary: The compromise hit a third-party processor rather than the agency's own systems, the latest case of a state government's outsourced platform leaking residents' identity documents. Affected license holders face heightened identity-theft and phishing risk.

sources.title: Texas govt data breach exposes over 3 million driver's licenses

topics.title: Fake Reputation Pushes Crypto-Stealing Malware
topics.latest_developments: Check Point researchers exposed a campaign that inflated GitHub activity, planted software reviews and YouTube tutorials, and seeded favorable VirusTotal comments to make malicious cryptocurrency sniper bots and gambling predictors look trustworthy.
topics.summary: The attackers packaged the malware as money-making tools and manufactured a veneer of legitimacy across the platforms victims trust to vet software. Users who installed the bots and predictors instead surrendered their cryptocurrency.

sources.title: Cybercriminals abused GitHub, YouTube and VirusTotal to push crypto-stealing malware

weather: Tonight: Isolated Rain Showers then Mostly Clear, low 57F.
Saturday: Mostly Sunny then Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 78F.

business.title: Schwebel's to Liquidate After 120 Years
business.latest_developments: Schwebel Baking Company announced Friday it will wind down operations and pursue a formal liquidation in the coming weeks.
business.summary: Youngstown-based Schwebel Baking Company, whose bread fills grocery shelves across western Pennsylvania, blamed aging plants and equipment, costly labor contracts, and years of financial strain for the shutdown.

sources.title: Schwebel Baking Company shutting down after 120 years
Schwebel Baking Company will close all operations this summer

business.title: P3R Chief Executive Departs
business.latest_developments: Troy Schooley will step down as head of P3R to launch his own sports-management firm.
business.summary: P3R, the Pittsburgh event-management organization that runs the annual Pittsburgh Marathon and helped stage the 2026 NFL Draft, loses Troy Schooley after more than a decade shaping the city's running and sports-event calendar.

sources.title: P3R CEO Steps Down to Start New Sports Management Firm

around_town.title: Citizen Science Lab Opens on Herron Avenue
around_town.latest_developments: The Citizen Science Lab opened its renovated home along the Herron Avenue corridor.
around_town.summary: The new Citizen Science Lab building adds laboratories, meeting spaces, and an attached greenhouse, which backers hope will lift the Herron Avenue corridor.

sources.title: Citizen Science Lab's new home brings hope to Herron Avenue corridor

around_town.title: Detective Launches Youth Horseback Program
around_town.latest_developments: Detective Dorothea Leftwich launched 'Riding Into the Future,' a free horseback-riding and leadership program for Pittsburgh children.
around_town.summary: Leftwich, the Pittsburgh Bureau of Police's first Black female mounted patrol detective, built the program to give inner-city kids access to horses and mentorship.

sources.title: Pittsburgh police mounted patrol officer brings horseback riding to inner-city children

around_town.title: Heavy Rain Expected Monday
around_town.latest_developments: WTAE meteorologists flagged Monday, June 22, as an impact day for heavy rain across western Pennsylvania.
around_town.summary: Isolated showers linger through the weekend before a wetter system arrives Monday, prompting the impact-day designation.

sources.title: Pittsburgh: Impact Day Monday for heavy rain

events.title: Western PA Juneteenth Celebration
events.latest_developments: The four-day Juneteenth festival is underway downtown through Sunday, June 21.
events.summary: The Western PA Juneteenth and Black Music Celebration, billed as North America's largest Juneteenth festival, runs Thursday through Sunday, June 18-21, from 11 a.m. to 10 p.m. across Point State Park, Market Square, and Liberty Avenue, presented free by Stop the Violence Pittsburgh.

sources.title: 14+ things to do this weekend, from Juneteenth and Monster Jam to Summer Splash and Bubblesburgh

events.title: World Cup Fan Zone at Acrisure Stadium
events.latest_developments: DJ Pauly D joined the July 5 bill alongside country singer Brett Young for the free World Cup Fan Zone.
events.summary: The free Pennsylvania World Cup Fan Zone at Acrisure Stadium on Pittsburgh's North Shore stages performances by DJ Pauly D and Brett Young on Sunday, July 5.

sources.title: DJ Pauly D joins Brett Young on bill for World Cup Fan Zone at Acrisure Stadium

around_teams.title: Pirates Trade Joey Bart
around_teams.latest_developments: The Pirates dealt catcher Joey Bart to the Braves for reliever Hunter Stratton, adding bullpen help and clearing their catching logjam.
around_teams.summary: Post-Gazette beat writers report the trade reshapes the catching depth chart and addresses a bullpen the team has struggled to repair, while a follow-up mailbag weighs the path forward at catcher and whether Marcell Ozuna returns.

sources.title: The Pirates trade Joey Bart, adding bullpen help and solving their logjam at catcher
Pirates mailbag: What's the plan at catcher after the Joey Bart trade? Is Marcell Ozuna back?

around_teams.title: Steelers Offseason Chat
around_teams.latest_developments: Post-Gazette beat writer Christopher Carter fielded questions on the Steelers' roster heading toward training camp in his June 18 chat.
around_teams.summary: Carter took reader questions on cornerback Joey Porter Jr., edge rusher T.J. Watt, cornerback Jalen Ramsey, quarterback Aaron Rodgers, and coach Mike McCarthy as the team approaches camp at Saint Vincent College.

sources.title: Christopher Carter's Steelers chat transcript: 06.18.26

team_usa.title: Pulisic Out vs Australia
team_usa.latest_developments: A calf injury ruled Christian Pulisic out of the U.S. men's World Cup group match against Australia on Friday, with Ricardo Pepi starting in his place.
team_usa.summary: The United States, co-hosting the 2026 World Cup, lost captain Christian Pulisic for the group-stage clash with Australia at Lumen Field in Seattle.

sources.title: Pulisic (calf) out of USMNT's clash with Australia

team_usa.title: Jenny Simpson Improving After Collapse
team_usa.latest_developments: U.S. Olympic 1,500-meter bronze medalist Jenny Simpson showed 'encouraging improvement' after collapsing while pacing a mile group at a Raleigh event this week.
team_usa.summary: Simpson, a former world champion in the 1,500 meters, remained under medical care Thursday following the collapse.

sources.title: Simpson improving after collapsing at track event

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 2)
reading.summary: Continuing his Silicon Valley bubble series, Zitron argues OpenAI and Anthropic are racing to public markets for exit liquidity while burning billions with no path to profit, pointing to OpenAI's roughly $34 billion in spending against about $13 billion in revenue.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Michael Morton About E-Commerce in the Age of AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Michael Morton on how AI reshapes e-commerce, covering unfalsifiable bear cases, distribution versus referral models, grocery, and autonomous vehicles.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn't Breaking Work. It's Already Broken.
reading.summary: Reacting to a Financial Times interview with the Work AI Institute's Rebecca Hinds and a survey of 6,000 digital workers, Newport argues AI exposes dysfunction that already plagued knowledge work rather than creating it.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
